name: Misty Twinkle
description: The tool is an interactive air quality modeling dashboard that compares two alternative methods for reducing methane emissions at a municipal solid waste landfill. It takes site conditions (wind speed, stack height, and atmospheric stability). It runs a Gaussian plume dispersion model to estimate ground-level CH4 concentrations downwind of the landfill before and after the two alternative options. The alternatives I have chosen to compare are active gas collection and passive compost biofilter. The model assumes a level of efficiency that is explained in the assumptions. From the chosen site conditions, the outputs include concentration-distance plots, receptor comparisons at fixed downwind distances, and a wind speed sensitivity analysis. Finally, a multi-criteria decision analysis tab scores both alternatives across technical effectiveness, cost, public health, and environmental impact to support a weighted comparison between the two models.
